mapping.a_element: distinct tags accumulating as the system grows in 'time'
mapping.b_element: occupied lattice sites accumulating as mass/particles n grows

brief_justification: Only a generic 'a quantity grows with a scale parameter' overlap exists; the machinery differs entirely. Paper A is an empirical statistical scaling law (sub-linear power-law / Heaps-type vocabulary growth from a stochastic innovation/cognitive process), whereas Paper B is a deterministic discrete-Laplacian aggregation (rotor-router / abelian sandpile) with a geometric shape theorem proving the occupied region converges to a Euclidean ball via inradius/outradius bounds — no role-for-role correspondence between an innovation counting process and a lattice-growth potential-theory argument holds.
